title: 2024 U.S. Presidential Election
narrative: The 2024 U.S. presidential election generated the highest-volume markets in Polymarket's history. Subsequent reporting by Mitts and Ofir (2026) and others identified pre-election positioning patterns consistent with informed trading, particularly in the final weeks of the cycle. The four top-line markets (Trump wins, Harris wins, Other Republican, Michelle Obama) collectively traded over $2.96 billion in volume.

primary_sources.note: Coverage of Polymarket election volume and post-election analysis

academic_sources.section: Composite informed-trading screen, $143M aggregate anomalous profit estimate

markets.label: Donald Trump wins 2024 U.S. Presidential Election
Kamala Harris wins 2024 U.S. Presidential Election
Other Republican wins 2024 U.S. Presidential Election
Michelle Obama wins 2024 U.S. Presidential Election

notes: Full market_id hexadecimal hashes are recoverable via Polymarket gamma-api search by question text. Trade history for these markets is not currently retrievable through the public Polymarket subgraph due to indexer-capacity limits at this volume tier. Alternative recovery paths are documented in Nechepurenko (2026) Section 6.6.

title: October 2024 Iran strike on Israel
narrative: Hours before Iran launched ballistic missiles at Israel on October 1, 2024, six newly-created Polymarket wallets purchased YES shares on Iran-strike contracts at prices as low as ten cents. The wallets collectively realized approximately $1.2 million when markets resolved YES hours later. This is the canonical insider-trading case study cited in Mitts and Ofir (2026) and reflects the deadline-contract structure characteristic of documented Polymarket insider activity.

primary_sources.note: Initial reporting on suspicious pre-strike trading

markets.label: Iran strike on Israel today
Another Iran strike on Israel by Friday
Iran strike on Israel by November 8

notes: The first market resolved YES on the day of the strike (October 1, 2024); the two follow-on markets were deadline contracts that resolved NO. Post-resolution wallet-clustering analysis identified six newly-created wallets responsible for the bulk of YES purchases prior to the strike.

title: 2026 U.S.-Iran Conflict Cluster
narrative: Beginning in late January 2026, a cluster of seven Polymarket markets formed around a potential U.S.-Iran military escalation. Markets included strike-by-date contracts, ceasefire-by-date contracts, leadership-change-by-date contracts, and the broader 'US forces enter Iran' contract. The cluster traded $832.5M in cumulative volume. Several of these markets exhibit price trajectories and wallet patterns flagged by Mitts and Ofir's (2026) composite screen as candidates for informed trading, particularly during the late-March escalation phase.

primary_sources.note: Coverage of US-Iran tensions and Polymarket trading

markets.label: US forces enter Iran by April 30
markets.trade_history_blocker: subgraph_indexer_capacity_exceeded; price history available via CLOB endpoint

markets.label: US x Iran ceasefire by April 7
markets.trade_history_blocker: subgraph_indexer_capacity_exceeded; price history available via CLOB endpoint

markets.label: Khamenei out by February 28
Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire by April 18
US strikes Iran by February 28
Khamenei out by March 31

notes: Trade history for the two highest-volume markets in this cluster (US forces enter Iran, US-Iran ceasefire) exceeds the public Polymarket subgraph's indexer envelope. Price history for both is available via the CLOB prices-history endpoint; full trade-level data requires Dune Analytics. The remaining four markets in this cluster are subgraph-recoverable. The April 30 escalation deadline is associated with a documented public event on April 3 (F-15E rescue operation) that triggered YES resolution on April 9.

title: January 2026 U.S.–Venezuela / Maduro operation cluster
narrative: Operation Absolute Resolve (January 3, 2026): U.S. Special Forces conducted a capture operation in Venezuela resulting in Nicolás Maduro being taken into U.S. custody. Following President Trump's public announcement, Polymarket resolved multiple related markets to YES. The cluster is the first documented Polymarket insider-trading case involving classified pre-decisional military information, as alleged in the DOJ indictment of U.S. Army soldier Gannon Van Dyke (April 23, 2026). Van Dyke is alleged to have used access to classified operational planning documents to purchase YES shares in multiple prediction markets shortly before the operation was executed.

primary_sources.note: DOJ indictment of Gannon Van Dyke — first insider-trading case on a prediction market involving classified U.S. military information
Operation Absolute Resolve — Maduro capture coverage

markets.label: Maduro in US custody by January 31
US x Venezuela military engagement by January 15
US x Venezuela military engagement by January 31
Trump invokes War Powers against Venezuela by January 9
Trump invokes War Powers against Venezuela by January 31
US x Venezuela military engagement by March 31
Nicolás Maduro seen in public by January 5
US invades Venezuela by January 31 (Van Dyke traded YES; resolved NO)
US x Venezuela military engagement by December 31 (deadline-miss; op Jan 3)
US x Venezuela military engagement by November 30 (deadline-miss)
US forces in Venezuela again by January 31 (second-incursion; resolved NO)

notes: DOJ indictment uses simplified market names: 'Maduro out by January 31, 2026' maps to Polymarket's 'Maduro in U.S. custody by January 31?' (YES, $11M); 'US forces in Venezuela by January 31, 2026' maps to 'US x Venezuela military engagement by January 31, 2026?' (YES, $931k). The 'US forces in Venezuela again by January 31' market ($570k, NO) asks about a second incursion and is distinct from the DOJ-referenced YES market. 'Will the U.S. invade Venezuela by January 31, 2026?' ($8.4M) resolved NO because the 'military offensive to establish territorial control' standard was not met by the capture operation; Van Dyke allegedly purchased YES shares but the position resolved against him.

title: Bitcoin ETF SEC approval
narrative: On January 10, 2024, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission approved spot Bitcoin ETFs. The Polymarket contract on whether approval would occur by January 15, 2024 traded approximately $12.6M in volume in the run-up. The market is a canonical example of a regulatory-decision deadline contract; pre-decision price movement reflects the market's incorporation of leaked or anticipated SEC announcements.

markets.label: Bitcoin ETF approved by January 15

notes: Single-market case. Trade-level analysis is feasible; the regulatory-decision category is under-represented in the broader inventory and this case is the cleanest available example.

title: Google Year-in-Search rankings 2025
narrative: Google publishes its annual 'Year in Search' rankings in early December. In December 2025, three Polymarket markets traded on whether specific names (Gene Hackman, Ismail Haniyeh, Zendaya) would appear in the rankings. Public reporting documented unusual pre-publication trading patterns; the rankings are a corporate proprietary disclosure and pre-publication knowledge would constitute material non-public information. Cumulative volume across the three markets was approximately $5.3M.

academic_sources.section: Section 4 supplementary cases: corporate proprietary disclosures

markets.label: Gene Hackman #1 in Passings category
markets.trade_history_blocker: Limited trade history; subgraph backfill recovered partial coverage

markets.label: Ismail Haniyeh #1 in Passings category
Zendaya #1 in Actors category

notes: Year-in-Search rankings are produced internally by Google and are not pre-announced. Pre-publication price movement on these markets is consistent with information leakage from within Google's product organization or from contractor exposure.

title: FTX / SBF case cluster
narrative: The FTX collapse and Sam Bankman-Fried's prosecution generated a cluster of three Polymarket markets: presidential pardon by Biden, sentence length, and the timing of FTX customer payouts. The cluster includes both event-resolved contracts (sentencing) and deadline contracts (payouts). Cumulative volume was approximately $9.5M.

primary_sources.outlet: U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York

markets.label: Biden pardons SBF
SBF sentenced to 50+ years
FTX no payouts in 2024

notes: The SBF-sentenced market opened with p_open=0.97 (high consensus that 50+ years was unlikely) and is an example of the edge-effect regime described in Nechepurenko (2026) Section 4.3. The FTX-payouts market is deadline-resolved.

title: Romanian presidential election 2024-2025
narrative: The Romanian presidential election of late 2024 produced one of the most discussed Polymarket episodes of the cycle: the contract on Nicolae Ciuca winning traded at near-99% YES probability throughout its lifetime, but the election was annulled by the Constitutional Court in early December 2024 amid allegations of foreign interference, and the market resolved NO. Volume reached approximately $326M. The market is an example of the surprise-resolved regime: extreme price-vs-outcome divergence with near-zero ILS by construction. The market is included for completeness as a documented case in the public-reporting record despite its analytical limitations under the present methodology.

primary_sources.note: Coverage of election annulment and Polymarket reaction

markets.label: Ciuca wins Romanian Presidential Election 2024

notes: Single-market case. Opened at p_open=0.991 (near-certainty YES) and resolved NO. ILS computed under the standard formula yields approximately zero by construction (price never moved); the market is in the trivial-resolution regime. Included in the inventory because the public-reporting record is unambiguous and the case is methodologically instructive as an edge case.
